titleStmt.title: Letter from Francis Sheehy Skeffington to Lillian Metge, 1 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Francis Sheehy Skeffington

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Francis Sheehy Skeffington to Lillian Metge regarding several political issues including conscription, suffrage and the anti-taxation movement. Francis anticipates a big move toward the anti-war taxation campaign as the next budget approaches.
Francis Sheehy Skeffington (1878-1916), journalist, was a prominent feminist, activist and co-founder of the Irish Women's Franchise league in 1908. In 1912, he and his wife, Hanna Sheehy Skeffington (1877-1946), founded the suffragist newspaper The Irish Citizen. A staunch pacifist and nationalist, Sheehy Skeffington opposed Irish involvement in the Great War. During the Easter Rising, he attempted to organise a civilian defence force to prevent looting and saved the life of a British officer under fire. Arrested on the evening of Tuesday 25 April 1916, Sheehy Skeffington and two other civilians were shot by firing squad on the morning of Wednesday 26 April.
Lillian M. Metge (1871-1954) was a militant suffragist, a member of the Women's Social and Political Union, and a contributor to the Irish Citizen.

msIdentifier.collection: Sheehy Skeffington Papers, Collection List No. 47; Ms 33,612/18-17

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
11, Grosvenor Place,
Rathmines, Dublin. 1—II.—16 Dear Mrs. Metge, Your letter and enclosures reached me duly. I am returning
the Agenda, which I was very glad to see. I will not, of course, make
any public use of it. Many thanks for your hints re societies to write to in England;
I am following this up. But there are so many things on hand here
that I see I shall not be able to visit England so soon as i had ex-
pected. Conscription being (for the present) disposed of, the next
big Irish campaign will be against taxation, — a demand that Ireland
shall be exempted from war-taxation, not being interested in the war
and being already taxed far in excess of the country's taxable capac-
ity. I expect a big movement on these lines to develop before and
after the next Budget; and the developments and possibilities are
very interesting. Sheehy Skeffington
